text: The standard General Emergency Alarm signal consists of:

options.text: One long blast followed by one short blast
Continuous ringing of the bell for 10 minutes
Seven or more short blasts followed by one prolonged blast on the ship's whistle/horn

explanation: The General Emergency Alarm (GEA) is standardized to prevent confusion during crises. It consists of at least seven or more short blasts followed by one prolonged blast on the ship’s whistle/siren and the same signal on the alarm bells, as required by the LSA Code.
